summary: Matched the common fighter dash attack action by moving the 0x8008B3E4-0x8008B638 assembly block out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into new C source/header files for ftCo_AttackDash. The PR added the new object to obj_files.mk, exposed prototypes and motion-variable layout needed by the C, and renamed several still-assembly local labels to global lbl_800D8xxx symbols so remaining asm and new C could call them safely. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice.

key_files.role: new matched C implementation for ftCo_AttackDash decision, enter, anim, IASA, physics, and collision callbacks
new public prototypes for ftCo_AttackDash_DecideMotion, Anim, SetMv0, IASA, Phys, and Coll
removed matched assembly block and converted several remaining local labels to global lbl_800D8xxx labels
added ftCo_AttackDashVars at fp+2340 as mv.co.attackdash.x0
expanded ftCommonData fields around offsets 0x50 and 0x68 used by dash attack physics and motion variable setup
typed ft_80085030 assembly signature for use from C
declared remaining lbl_800D8A38, lbl_800D8AE0, lbl_800D8B9C, and lbl_800D8C54 asm entry points
adjusted ftCo_Attack_800CCF58 prototype to be callable with enum_t

what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_AttackDash.c with ftCo_AttackDash_DecideMotion, ftCo_AttackDash_Anim, ftCo_AttackDash_SetMv0, ftCo_AttackDash_IASA, ftCo_AttackDash_Phys, and ftCo_AttackDash_Coll.

Removed the corresponding assembly for .L_8008B3E4, .L_8008B498, .L_8008B4D4, ftCo_AttackDash_Anim, .L_8008B584, ftCo_AttackDash_IASA, ftCo_AttackDash_Phys, and ftCo_AttackDash_Coll from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.

Implemented dash-attack decision logic based on A press, held LR, item presence, item state from it_8026B30C, light throw dash via ft_800957F4, item attack via ftCo_Attack_800CCF58, and normal fighter-specific dash attack entry.
Preserved Kirby-specific behavior in a helper that calls ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68 for FTKIND_KIRBY and otherwise enters ftCo_MS_AttackDash.
Implemented dash-attack entry by clearing fp->x2218_b0, calling Fighter_ChangeMotionState with ftCo_MS_AttackDash and Ft_MF_None, calling ftAnim_8006EBA4, and setting fp->mv.co.attackdash.x0 to 0.
Implemented ftCo_AttackDash_SetMv0 by assigning p_ftCommonData->x68 into fp->mv.co.attackdash.x0, with a MUST_MATCH-only unused stack workaround.
Implemented animation, IASA, physics, and collision callbacks with existing helpers: ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining/ft_8008A2BC, lbl_800D8AE0/ftCo_Wait_IASA, ft_80085030, and ft_80084104.
Added ftCo_AttackDashVars to ftCommon_MotionVars so fp+2340 can be referenced as mv.co.attackdash.x0.
Expanded ftCommonData placeholders to expose x50 and x68 floats needed by this matched code.
Renamed remaining assembly-only local labels .L_800D8A38, .L_800D8AE0, .L_800D8B9C, and .L_800D8C54 to global lbl_ symbols and updated branches/calls to them.
Added ftCo_AttackDash.c.o to obj_files.mk so the matched C replaces the removed assembly at link time.
smart_moves: Kept semantically unknown remaining assembly entry points as address-style lbl_800D8xxx names instead of guessing names, while still making them global for C/asm linkage.
Split the matched action into a dedicated ftCo_AttackDash.c/ftCo_AttackDash.h pair, matching the ftCommon action-file organization already used by neighboring actions such as ftCo_Attack1.
Introduced the exact motion-variable union member for fp+2340 rather than leaving raw offset access in the new C.
Typed ft_80085030 only as far as needed for the new call site, converting a void placeholder into a usable prototype without over-renaming the underlying asm routine.
Used a small switch for the item state case 2 path, preserving the assembly-like control flow for the ftCo_Attack_800CCF58(gobj, 4) branch.
Used a MUST_MATCH-only unused stack allocation in ftCo_AttackDash_SetMv0 to satisfy the original float-to-int/store stack pattern without polluting the normal source path.
decomp_lessons: When extracting an action from a large asm file, also update all remaining asm call sites from local .L labels to either named C functions or global lbl_ labels; otherwise cross-object calls will not link cleanly.
For fighter action code, model fp+2340 through ftCommon_MotionVars once its per-action meaning is known, even if the field is still named x0.
Expose only the ftCommonData offsets that the matched function needs, keeping unknown neighboring fields as UNK_T when semantics are not yet established.
If a helper remains in assembly and its purpose is uncertain, prefer lbl_<address> declarations in headers over speculative semantic names.
Small prototype fixes in adjacent headers are often required when moving code from asm to C, especially for functions previously declared static or with placeholder signatures.
MUST_MATCH conditionals can document and isolate artificial stack-shape fixes, such as preserving an unused stack slot around fctiwz-style conversions.
naming_conventions: Public common fighter action functions use ftCo_AttackDash_* names, e.g. ftCo_AttackDash_DecideMotion, ftCo_AttackDash_Anim, ftCo_AttackDash_IASA, ftCo_AttackDash_Phys, and ftCo_AttackDash_Coll.
Action-specific motion variables are nested under mv.co.attackdash with field x0 when semantics are still unknown.
Unknown but exported assembly labels are named lbl_800D8A38, lbl_800D8AE0, lbl_800D8B9C, and lbl_800D8C54 rather than .L_ local labels.
Private C helpers can use descriptive local names such as decideFighter and doEnter for internal control-flow factoring.
Header include guards for address-based files continue using GALE01_<address>, as in GALE01_08B3E4 for ftCo_AttackDash.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the 0x8008B3E4-0x8008B638 assembly region with C while leaving later ft_08A4.s assembly intact.
Added ftCo_AttackDash.c.o immediately before asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.o in obj_files.mk, preserving object order around the removed text region.
Removed sdata2 constants .L_804D8460 and .L_804D8464 after the C version used NULL, 0, and 1 literals for Fighter_ChangeMotionState arguments.
Used explicit bool returns in ftCo_AttackDash_DecideMotion to mirror the original li r3, 1 / li r3, 0 paths.
Used the MUST_MATCH unused u8 stack array in ftCo_AttackDash_SetMv0 to reproduce stack behavior around assigning p_ftCommonData->x68 into an int motion variable.
Updated assembly call operands from .L_8008B3E4 to ftCo_AttackDash_DecideMotion and from .L_8008B584 to ftCo_AttackDash_SetMv0 in Dash, Run, and RunDirect IASA paths.

review_feedback.details: The PR context reports zero issue comments, zero review comments, and zero reviews; no human feedback was available to extract.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_800D8A38, lbl_800D8AE0, lbl_800D8B9C, or lbl_800D8C54 to semantic names.
Search for later refinement of ftCommonData fields x50 and x68 used by dash attack.
Search for later PRs that improve ftCo_AttackDashVars.x0 naming or type.
Compare other ftCommon action matches that use MUST_MATCH unused stack workarounds for float-to-int conversions.
Find subsequent cleanup of ftCo_Attack_800CCF58 argument type or enum values.
